heading: Applicability to United States intelligence activities of Federal laws implementing international treaties and agreements
label: 3231 Applicability to United States intelligence activities of Federal laws implementing international treaties and agreements
notes: CodificationSection was formerly classified to prior to editorial reclassification and renumbering as this section.section 442 of this title

children.heading: In general
children.label: a In general
children.text: No Federal law enacted on or after , that implements a treaty or other international agreement shall be construed as making unlawful an otherwise lawful and authorized intelligence activity of the United States Government or its employees, or any other person to the extent such other person is carrying out such activity on behalf of, and at the direction of, the United States, unless such Federal law specifically addresses such intelligence activity.December 27, 2000

children.heading: Authorized intelligence activities
children.label: b Authorized intelligence activities
children.text: An intelligence activity shall be treated as authorized for purposes of subsection (a) if the intelligence activity is authorized by an appropriate official of the United States Government, acting within the scope of the official duties of that official and in compliance with Federal law and any applicable Presidential directive.
